macro_summary: Rate backdrop (FRED, not live): payrolls +57k (cooling from +129k); unemployment 4.2% (down); last CPI 4.3% YoY (up, stale). U.S. stocks edged higher on Friday, with the S&P 500 adding 0.47% to 754.95 and the Nasdaq Composite rising 0.36%. WTI crude slipped 1.35% to $71.41 as U.S.-Iran talks offered brief relief, though the Hormuz crisis remains unresolved. The 10-year Treasury yield nudged up to 4.57% after Fed minutes revealed deep divisions over the inflation outlook — officials are now weighing both rate hikes and cuts depending on incoming data. The VIX tumbled to 15.03, signalling calm despite the looming CPI report and the start of semiconductor earnings next week.
commodities_note: WTI fell 1.35% to $71.41 on tentative peace overtures, but remains elevated relative to pre‑conflict levels. Gold eased 0.62% to $4,104.10, capped by firm yields amid Middle East uncertainty.

These actions suggest the hyperscaler‑in‑housing risk is materialising, breaking one of the bull‑case legs. Avoid until either (a) CRWV signs a durable, multi‑year contract with a major non‑Meta tenant, or (b) the stock falls far enough that the impaired thesis is fully discounted (likely below $80).

singapore.summary: All three Singapore banks are severely overbought after a months‑long rally, with RSI readings above 80 and extensions >11% above the 50‑day. UOB stands out as the only one trading at a discount to the cluster median P/E, but the dual headwinds of high US rates and a strong S$NEER mean NIM relief remains elusive.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally bearish until rate or FX headwinds ease; technicals extremely extended.
singapore.key_development: No new banking news this week; the cluster remains in a hard‑blocked state.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster is split: NVDA reclaimed its 50‑day, but AMD, MU, and SNDK are extended, while LITE and AVGO are blocked on momentum. ASML and TSMC earnings next week are the make‑or‑break catalysts. Memory stocks have been volatile, with SK Hynix’s US debut adding liquidity but not stability.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Mixed — AI capex theme intact, but valuations and extensions are stretched ahead of earnings.
semiconductors.key_development: Meta’s AI chip production in September (per Reuters) reinforces custom‑ASIC demand, a positive for AVGO and a headwind for merchant GPU names like NVDA and AMD.

big_tech.summary: AMZN, GOOG, and MSFT are all below their 50‑day averages with no compelling entry triggers. MSFT’s Copilot tie‑up with GPT 5.6 reinforces its OpenAI dependency, while GOOG faces a neutral pre‑earnings setup. No cluster‑wide catalyst until the earnings season begins in late July.
big_tech.thesis_status: Structurally sound but tactically stalled — wait for earnings catalysts.
big_tech.key_development: OpenAI’s GPT 5.6 integration cements MSFT’s dependence, clouding the Azure revenue‑quality story.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: NVDA’s $2B photonics investment and the AI optical interconnect boom directly benefit COHR; its 6‑inch InP yield struggles are a key differentiator vs LITE.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if COHR pulls back 10‑15% from current levels on photonics‑cycle concerns.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Marvell’s 5M coherent PIC shipment milestone confirms the AI‑optics demand ramp, linking to LITE and TSEM photonics themes.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after LITE’s next earnings for a coordinated signal on optical‑interconnect demand.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: Arista’s 1.6T switch platform is gaining traction with Meta and Microsoft (per Simply Wall St.); NVDA Spectrum‑X adoption ripples through the networking supply chain.
interconnected.entry_note: Monitor ANET on any pullback to the 50‑day; its AI switching exposure complements NOK’s networking thesis.

interconnected.name: Samsung Electronics
interconnected.reason: Samsung’s HBM3E qualification struggles benefit SK Hynix and Micron’s duopoly, making it a key read‑through for memory ASP strength.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Samsung announces a major HBM design‑win that threatens the duopoly.

active_risks: US‑Iran military exchanges continue in the Strait of Hormuz, with oil tanker traffic at near‑standstill despite on‑again/off‑again peace talks (Fox News, Jul 9).
Fed officials are deeply divided on the inflation outlook, with rate hikes back on the table if June CPI comes in hot (KITCO, Jul 10).
China temporarily bans helium exports, a critical gas for chip manufacturing, underscoring broadening tech‑supply‑chain weaponisation (NY Post, Jul 10).
Corporate America’s heavy dependence on Chinese rare‑earths and materials is flagged as a national‑security vulnerability (Fox News, Jul 9).
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil at $71 is well off the spike highs but remains elevated, keeping energy‑cost fears alive for chipmakers. Semiconductor earnings season begins with ASML and TSMC next week — any negative surprises would compound the already strained AI‑capex narrative. The VIX at 15 suggests complacency, despite the open‑ended Hormuz crisis and the looming CPI report.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: China’s helium export ban (NY Post, Jul 10) adds a new supply‑chain risk for semiconductor manufacturing. Fed minutes revealed unprecedented division over the rate path (KITCO).

base.description: Hormuz stalemate holds, oil remains near $70. Fed stays on hold, CPI shows modest cooling. Semis grind sideways into earnings; NVDA holds the 50‑day while extended names (AMD, MU) correct. Singapore banks remain blocked.

optimistic.description: Ceasefire is restored; WTI drops below $65. CPI surprises low, extinguishing rate‑hike bets. Tech and semis rally, lifting NVDA, and TSMC beats on earnings. Singapore banks begin to cool off, opening entries.

pessimistic.description: Hormuz conflict escalates, oil spikes above $85. CPI hot, forcing a Fed rate hike. Semis sell off sharply; ASML/TSMC earnings disappoint. All extended names crash back to their 200‑day averages.

wildcard.description: Trump announces a surprise comprehensive deal with Iran that lifts all sanctions. Oil crashes to $55, yields plummet, and a massive risk‑on rally ensues — but the sustainability of such a pivot is questionable.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold cash and wait for CPI and the ASML/TSMC earnings gauntlet next week. Use any post‑earnings pullback to accumulate high‑conviction names at lower prices.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Cautiously defensive ahead of CPI and semiconductor earnings
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 7 of 29 names are hard‑blocked on technical extension, and three of the four BigTech names are in no‑action holds. The only near‑term WATCH setups are BE (post‑RCP graduation), NVDA (reclaiming 50‑day), and SK Hynix (volatile). Risk/reward is unfavourable across the board until the macro picture clarifies.
